title: Delicate Steamed Korean Pork Belly Banchan
description: A peppery and fluffy Korean dish featuring pork belly slices, perfect for a midnight craving, originating from the Gangwon region. This low-fat-friendly banchan is steamed to perfection in a cataplana vessel.
instructions: Slice the pork belly into 1/4 inch thick pieces and set aside.
In a small bowl, mix together Korean chili flakes, black pepper, garlic, and green onions.
In a cataplana vessel, arrange a layer of the pork belly slices.
Drizzle a small amount of sesame oil over the pork belly slices.
Sprinkle a portion of the chili flake mixture over the pork belly slices.
Repeat the layering process until all ingredients are used, ending with a layer of the chili flake mixture on top.
Pour soy sauce and rice vinegar over the top layer.
Steam the banchan in the cataplana vessel over boiling water for 10 minutes.
Remove the cataplana from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
